question: An operator selects a mix of `orderProcess` instances in Operate for migration to a new version. Which subset of that selection will be silently excluded from the migration plan?

options.text: Completed and canceled instances — only active or incident instances are part of the plan.
Instances currently in an incident state, because incidents must be resolved first.
Instances still actively waiting at user tasks for human input.
Instances of the latest deployed version of `orderProcess`.

a.text: The docs say it is only possible to migrate running process instances (active or incident state); all other process instances will not be part of the migration plan and will be ignored.

b.text: Incident instances are explicitly considered running and are included; they do not need to be resolved before migration.

c.text: Active instances waiting at user tasks are still running and are part of the migration plan.

d.text: There is no rule that excludes instances of the latest version; eligibility is by run state, not by version recency.

explanation: Operate keeps migration safe by considering only running instances — those in an active or incident state. Anything completed or cancelled is treated as immutable history, dropped from the migration plan, and silently ignored, even if the operator selected it.
